name: Whispers in the Deep
description: Strange sounds emanate from the ritual chamber beneath the manor. The dead are not silent — they are waiting.

DISCOVERED.journal_entry: Old Maren says she hears voices from beneath the earth — the spirits of those sacrificed to the forest. They're calling from the ritual chamber under the manor. She says they want to be heard.

ACTIVE.journal_entry: I'm heading into the ritual chamber beneath the manor. Old Maren gave me a bundle of dried thornleaf to burn — she says the smoke will let me see the spirits. If they want to be seen.
objectives: Enter the ritual chamber beneath the manor

CHAMBER_ENTERED.journal_entry: The chamber is cold. Ancient. The altar at its center is stained dark with decades of offerings. When I burned the thornleaf, the shadows began to move.

SPIRITS_ENCOUNTERED.journal_entry: They're here. Dozens of shapes in the smoke — men, women, children. All taken by the pact. One steps forward. I recognize the face from the compass portrait. Silas Maren.
objectives: Decide how to respond to the spirits

COMPLETED_COMMUNION.journal_entry: Silas spoke through the smoke. His voice was roots grinding against stone. He told me the Heartwood is not the enemy — it is a wound. The forest is in pain, and the pact is a tourniquet that's killing the patient. He showed me a path through the roots that bypasses the Warden.

COMPLETED_COMBAT.journal_entry: The spirits were hostile — twisted by decades of rage and pain. I fought shadow incarnations of the sacrificed. They dissolved into ash when struck. The chamber is quiet now. But I learned nothing.

COMPLETED_ALTAR_DESTROYED.journal_entry: The spirits begged me to destroy the altar. I did — cracked the stone with the heaviest thing I could find. The chamber shuddered. Something far below screamed. The pact's anchor point in the village is severed.

COMPLETED_VISION.journal_entry: The spirits showed me a vision: the forest as it was before the pact. Green, alive, welcoming. Then the first Ashworth lord cutting the first deal — 'blood for bounty.' The forest didn't want blood. It wanted communion. Conversation. The pact was a mistranslation. Thirty years of death for a misunderstanding.
